perturbation.original_text: 6. FINANCIALS (a) HCI shall pay MLX a guaranteed fixed sum of [*] US per annum during the Initial Term of this Agreement to be paid out as a monthly payment of [*] US (the "Monthly Payment"). This Monthly Payment shall be due by the 10th day of each month;
perturbation.changed_text: 6. FINANCIALS (a) HCI shall pay MLX a guaranteed fixed sum of [*] US per annum during the Initial Term of this Agreement to be paid out as a monthly payment of [*] US (the "Monthly Payment"). This Monthly Payment shall be due by the 10th day of each month; however, HCI can delay the payment until the 25th of the month, without incurring any late payment penalties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a clause that allows HCI to delay payment until the 25th of the month without penalties, contradicting the original payment deadline of the 10th of each month. This creates uncertainty regarding the actual due date and potential late fees, as one part of the same clause now offers conflicting terms. This uncertainty may make a third party question the actual deadline and payment terms of the agreement. This is also an addition so there will be no prior contradicting text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. FINANCIALS (a) HCI shall pay MLX a guaranteed fixed sum of [*] US per annum during the Initial Term of this Agreement to be paid out as a monthly payment of [*] US (the "Monthly Payment"). This Monthly Payment shall be due by the 10th day of each month; however, HCI can delay the payment until the 25th of the month, without incurring any late payment penalties; (b) HCI shall be entitled to all net revenue generated from advertising and e- commerce transactions generated by Canadian companies that may take place on the Co-Branded Site up to and including [*] US in any given year. Thereafter, any net revenues exceeding [*] US shall be shared fifty-fifty between the parties (50% MediaLinx--50% HCI). HCI shall be entitled to all proceeds generated from US companies on the Co-Branded Site. "Net revenue" is defined as revenue less: 1) third party expenses directly related to the acquisition of the revenue; for example, advertising broker commissions and advertising management services, and 2) internally paid commissions for selling the advertisement and/or sponsorship;
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause allowing HCI to delay payment until the 25th of the month without penalties. This directly contradicts the original, and still present, clause stating that the "Monthly Payment shall be due by the 10th day of each month." This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the payment due date, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. The definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' applies because the same section of the contract provides conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: 11. TERM & TERMINATION 11.1 Term The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of two (2) years (the "Term"). The effective date of the start of the Term will be from the Launch Date. 11.2 Termination (a) Either Party may terminate this Agreement upon 30 days prior written notice to the other Party if 1) such other Party breaches any material term of this Agreement, unless such breach is cured within 30 days; or 2) if any of the terms of this Agreement that require mutual approval are not mutually agreed to;
perturbation.changed_text: 11. TERM & TERMINATION 11.1 Term The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of two (2) years (the "Term"). The effective date of the start of the Term will be from the Launch Date. 11.2 Termination (a) Either Party may terminate this Agreement upon 30 days prior written notice to the other Party if 1) such other Party breaches any material term of this Agreement, unless such breach is cured within 30 days; or 2) if any of the terms of this Agreement that require mutual approval are not mutually agreed to; however, HCI can terminate this agreement at any time, without prior notice or penalty.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in the termination clause. Initially, either party could terminate with 30 days' notice for a material breach or lack of mutual agreement. The addition allows HCI to terminate 'at any time, without prior notice or penalty,' directly conflicting with the original 30-day notice requirement for both parties. This creates uncertainty about the actual termination terms applicable to HCI, making the clause ambiguous and potentially leading to disputes over the validity of a termination. This is also an addition so there will be no prior contradicting text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. TERM & TERMINATION 11.1 Term The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of two (2) years (the "Term"). The effective date of the start of the Term will be from the Launch Date. 11.2 Termination (a) Either Party may terminate this Agreement upon 30 days prior written notice to the other Party if 1) such other Party breaches any material term of this Agreement, unless such breach is cured within 30 days; or 2) if any of the terms of this Agreement that require mutual approval are not mutually agreed to; however, HCI can terminate this agreement at any time, without prior notice or penalty; (b) This Agreement may be terminated by a Party immediately, without notice: (i) upon the institution by or against the other Party of insolvency, receivership, or bankruptcy proceedings or any other proceedings for the settlement of the other Party's debts, if such proceedings are not dismissed within sixty (60) days; (ii) upon the other Party making an assignment for the benefit of creditors; or (iii) upon the other Party's dissolution;
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within section 11.2. Initially, both parties were subject to a 30-day written notice requirement for termination under specific circumstances. The added clause grants HCI the right to terminate 'at any time, without prior notice or penalty,' which directly conflicts with the original 30-day notice provision applicable to both parties. This creates an inconsistency concerning the termination terms applicable to HCI.

perturbation.original_text: 24. SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS Except as provided in this Section, neither Party may assign its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party shall be permitted to assign this Agreement and any of its rights and obligations hereunder to an affiliate or related company or to a purchaser of all or substantially all of its Internet business, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 24. SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS Except as provided in this Section, neither Party may assign its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party shall be permitted to assign this Agreement and any of its rights and obligations hereunder to an affiliate or related company or to a purchaser of all or substantially all of its Internet business, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party. HCI can, at any time, assign its rights to whomever, in which case, MediaLinx's consent is required, but can't be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause states neither party can assign rights without consent, unless it's to an affiliate or purchaser. The addition allows HCI to assign to anyone with MediaLinx's consent (which can't be unreasonably withheld), contradicting the initial restriction and creating uncertainty about the process and limitations of assignment, as well as for whom. The change introduces a special condition for one party that is not extended to the other. This is also an addition so there will be no prior contradicting text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 24. SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS Except as provided in this Section, neither Party may assign its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party shall be permitted to assign this Agreement and any of its rights and obligations hereunder to an affiliate or related company or to a purchaser of all or substantially all of its Internet business, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party. HCI can, at any time, assign its rights to whomever, in which case, MediaLinx's consent is required, but can't be unreasonably withheld. 25. FORCE MAJEURE Neither Party shall be liable for failure to perform or delay in performing any obligation under this Agreement if such failure or delay is due to fire, flood, earthquake, strike, war (declared or undeclared), embargo, blockade, legal prohibition, governmental action, riot, insurrection, damage, destruction or any other similar cause beyond the control of such Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that permits HCI to assign its rights to anyone with MediaLinx's consent, which cannot be unreasonably withheld. This directly contradicts the initial statement in the original and contradicted section, which states that "neither Party may assign its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party", except to an affiliate or purchaser. The new clause creates an exception for HCI, introducing an inconsistency in assignment rights. The special condition for HCI is not extended to MediaLinx, making the terms contradictory and creating uncertainty about the assignment process.
